title: Dryopteris costata
intro: Dryopteris costata, also known as the frigid buckler fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris and is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris costata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dryopteris costata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Dryopteris costata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Dryopteris is a group of ferns commonly known as the golden- or lady ferns.
